name: Tom Aspinall

qualityWinLedger.context: Top-five heavyweight and interim-title rematch win.
Top-ten heavyweight submission that begins the reviewed prime.
Ranked heavyweight return win after the knee injury.
Former champion name discounted for late-career timing.
Early UFC heavyweight win with limited quality value.

apexPeakDetail.notes: Back-to-back first-round interim-title knockouts establish an elite heavyweight apex; undisputed title proof remains incomplete because the Gane defense ended in a no contest.

shortCase: Aspinall is the heavyweight speed-and-finishing case: an 8-1 UFC record with one no contest, eight finishes, two interim-title wins, and no normal competitive loss inside his reviewed prime.
bestArgument: Aspinall's case starts with perfect UFC finishing efficiency, rare heavyweight speed, and title-level first-round wins over Pavlovich and Blaydes.
counterArgument: The argument against Aspinall is volume. Two title-fight wins and a little over four active elite years are not yet enough to match the established heavyweight champions.
whyRankedHere: Aspinall ranks here because his UFC case already has championship proof, elite heavyweight wins, a perfect finish rate, and one of the most explosive prime performances in the division's history.
whyNotHigher: He does not rank higher because the championship volume and active elite window are still short. He has two UFC title-fight wins, no completed undisputed title defense, and fewer top-five wins than the deepest heavyweight resumes.
finalTakeaway: Aspinall is already a serious UFC heavyweight peak case: fast, technically complete, title-proven, and perfect as a finisher. He needs completed undisputed defenses and more elite wins to become a top-tier all-time heavyweight resume.
